steps.title: Use slope-intercept form
steps.content: A linear function can be written as $f(x)=mx+b$, where $m$ is the slope and $b=f(0)$.

steps.title: Substitute the values
steps.content: The slope is $52$, so $m=52$. The value $f(0)=7$ means the $y$-intercept is $7$, so $b=7$. Therefore $$f(x)=52x+7.$$

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Choice A (‘$f(x) = 7x$’) has intercept $0$, choice B (‘$f(x) = 7x + 7$’) has slope $7$, and choice C (‘$f(x) = 52x$’) has intercept $0$. Only the credited option (‘$f(x) = 52x + 7$’) has slope $52$ and $f(0)=7$. D is correct.
